prompt: Create a complete receiver chain element set for an rfbudget: LNA (Gain=15dB, NF=2dB, OIP3=35dBm), bandpass filter using rffilter (Butterworth, order 5, passband 2.3-2.5 GHz), downconverting mixer using modulator (Gain=-6dB, NF=10dB, OIP3=20dBm, LO=2.1GHz, ConverterType=Down), IF amplifier (Gain=20dB, NF=4dB, OIP3=30dBm), and a 6dB attenuator. Build an rfbudget at 2.4GHz, -30dBm, 10MHz and print the system gain and NF.
expected_output: Code creating amplifier, rffilter, modulator, amplifier, attenuator elements with correct parameters, building rfbudget.

prompt: Set up a receiver link budget using rfantenna. Create a receive antenna (Type='Receiver', Gain=3dB, TxEIRP=30dBm, PathLoss=60dB) followed by an LNA (Gain=15dB, NF=2dB, OIP3=35dBm). Build an rfbudget at 2.4GHz, -30dBm (note: AvailableInputPower is ignored for Receiver antenna), 10MHz. Print the output power and SNR.
expected_output: Code using rfantenna with Type='Receiver' and correct TxEIRP/PathLoss, followed by amplifier in rfbudget.

prompt: Build an rfbudget receiver chain that includes passive elements: a DC blocking capacitor (seriesRLC with C=100pF), an LNA (Gain=15dB, NF=2dB, OIP3=35dBm), a shunt RF choke inductor (shuntRLC with L=100nH), and an LC ladder lowpass pi filter using lcladder('lowpasspi', [10e-9 10e-9], [1e-12 1e-12 1e-12], 'LPF'). Analyze at 2.4GHz, -30dBm, 10MHz and print the system gain.
expected_output: Code using seriesRLC, shuntRLC, lcladder, amplifier in rfbudget with correct parameters.

prompt: Create a txlineRLCGLine transmission line with LineLength=0.05m, R=0.5 Ohm/m, L=250e-9 H/m, C=100e-12 F/m, G=0 S/m. Extract S-parameters from 1 to 10 GHz (500 points) and plot S21 insertion loss and S11 return loss using tiledlayout. Print the S21 magnitude in dB at 5 GHz.
expected_output: Code using txlineRLCGLine with LineLength property, sparameters extraction, tiledlayout plotting.

prompt: Create an amplifier with Gain=15dB, NF=2dB, OIP3=35dBm, and Name='LNA'. Print all three properties to confirm they are set correctly.
expected_output: Code using amplifier('Gain', 15, 'NF', 2, 'OIP3', 35, 'Name', 'LNA') with correct name-value pairs.

prompt: Create an attenuator with 6 dB attenuation. Show that in an rfbudget, the noise figure automatically equals the attenuation value. Build a simple rfbudget with just the attenuator at 1GHz, 0dBm, 1MHz and print the NF.
expected_output: Code using attenuator('Attenuation', 6), building rfbudget, showing NF equals Attenuation automatically.

prompt: Create a phaseshift element with PhaseShift=45 degrees. Place it in a single-element rfbudget at 1GHz, 0dBm, 1MHz. Convert the budget to a circuit, extract S-parameters, and verify that the S21 phase angle is +45 degrees.
expected_output: Code using phaseshift('PhaseShift', 45), rfbudget, circuit(b), sparameters, checking angle(S21).

prompt: Create an rfantenna of Type='Receiver' with Gain=3dB, TxEIRP=30dBm, PathLoss=60dB. Print the properties. Explain that the received power is TxEIRP - PathLoss + Gain = -27 dBm.
Create a downconverting modulator with LO=2.1GHz and ConverterType='Down'. Place it in an rfbudget at 2.4GHz input, -30dBm, 10MHz. Print the OutputFrequency to show the IF = RF - LO = 0.3 GHz.
expected_output: Code using modulator with ConverterType='Down', rfbudget, printing OutputFrequency showing 0.3 GHz.

prompt: Create an rffilter with ResponseType='Bandpass', FilterType='Butterworth', FilterOrder=5, PassbandFrequency=[2.3e9 2.5e9]. Extract S-parameters from 1 to 4 GHz and verify the passband insertion loss is low (less than 1 dB at center frequency). Print the S21 magnitude in dB at 2.4 GHz.
expected_output: Code using rffilter with correct ResponseType (not FilterType='Bandpass'), extracting sparameters, checking passband loss.

prompt: Create a DC blocking capacitor using seriesRLC('C', 100e-12, 'Name', 'DCBlock'). Place it before an LNA (Gain=15dB, NF=2dB, OIP3=35dBm) in an rfbudget at 2.4GHz, -30dBm, 10MHz. Print the transducer gain to show the DC block passes RF with minimal loss.
expected_output: Code using seriesRLC for DC block in rfbudget chain.

prompt: Create an LC ladder lowpass pi filter using positional arguments: lcladder('lowpasspi', [10e-9 10e-9], [1e-12 1e-12 1e-12], 'LPF'). Note that lcladder uses positional constructor syntax, not name-value pairs. Print the filter name to confirm creation.
expected_output: Code using lcladder with positional args (topology, L, C, name), not NV pairs.

prompt: Create a txlineRLCGLine transmission line and set its physical length to 50mm. Use the LineLength property (not Length, which does not exist). Print the LineLength to confirm it is 0.05 m.
expected_output: Code using txlineRLCGLine with LineLength=0.05, not Length.

prompt: Build an rfbudget chain mixing passive and active elements: DC block (seriesRLC C=100pF), LNA (Gain=15dB, NF=2dB, OIP3=35dBm), and RF choke (shuntRLC L=100nH). Analyze at 2.4GHz, -30dBm, 10MHz. Print the gain at each stage to show the passive elements contribute near-zero insertion loss at RF.
expected_output: Code with seriesRLC + amplifier + shuntRLC in rfbudget, printing per-stage TransducerGain.

prompt: Design three rffilter objects: a 5th-order Butterworth bandpass (2.3-2.5 GHz), a 5th-order Chebyshev bandpass (2.3-2.5 GHz), and a 3rd-order Butterworth lowpass (3 GHz cutoff). Extract S-parameters for all three from 1 to 5 GHz and plot their S21 responses on the same axes in dB for comparison. Use a legend to identify each filter.
expected_output: Code creating three rffilter objects with different ResponseType/FilterType, extracting sparameters, plotting all S21 in dB on same axes with legend.

prompt: Build a bias tee network for an LNA: a DC blocking capacitor (seriesRLC, C=100pF) at the input, followed by a bias feed inductor (shuntRLC, L=100nH), then an LNA with 15 dB gain and 2 dB NF. Create an rfbudget at 2.4 GHz, -30 dBm, 10 MHz and print the cascaded gain and noise figure. Also extract S-parameters of just the bias tee passive elements (seriesRLC + shuntRLC) in a circuit object and plot S21.
expected_output: Code using seriesRLC for DC block and shuntRLC for bias inductor in rfbudget, plus a separate circuit with those elements to extract and plot S21.
